title: helicophyllum intortum
intro: Helicophyllum intortum, a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is also known as the 'Wild Celery' due to its celery-like appearance. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting overall well-being.
